secretary.role: Lane lifecycle coordinator â€” processes events, launches roles, maintains Ref<Map<string,LaneState>> (activeLanes). The single authority for mutating lane state.
secretary.state_storage: Ref<Map<string, LaneState>> â€” in-memory, no durable persistence beyond EventStore write-through

variants: ClaimsAcquired (files: readonly string[], claimIds: readonly string[])

event_name_map.description: Maps secretary RuntimeEvent tags to canonical EventName constants for EventStore write-through

state_role_mapping.description: Maps lane states to the role type that should be launched

role_output_to_event.description: Maps role completion to RuntimeEvent. For success status, dispatches by role. For blocked/critic-rejected, returns Blocked or CriticComplete(rejected). For failures, returns Failed.
role_output_to_event.note: RedTeamCompleted is NEVER emitted by roleOutputToEvent. It must come from a separate code path.

to_smevent.description: Maps secretary RuntimeEvent to state-machine RuntimeEvent (SMRuntimeEvent). Each variant maps to a {type, timestamp, payload} object.

critical_mappings.LearningComplete: "scout.completed" (same as ScoutComplete) â€” dual-path to scoped state

to_smevent.concern: toSMEvent uses RAW STRINGS like "scout.completed", "plan.produced", "redteam.finding" instead of EventName constants. evaluatePredicate uses EventName.* constants. They match by string value but are not type-linked.

processEvent_flow.description: 1. lookup lane via ensureLane. 2. write-through to EventStore via toStoreEvent. 3. convert to SMRuntimeEvent via toSMEvent. 4. call reduceCampaignState(smState, [smEvent], LANE_STATE_MACHINE). 5. If SM transitioned â†’ use smTarget as newState. If not â†’ handle Blockedâ†’blocked, Failedâ†’failed, Unblockedâ†’restore previous. 6. Add transition record + evidence to binder. 7. Update binder status. 8. Update activeLanes Ref.

handleRoleOutput_flow.description: 1. Converts RoleOutput to RuntimeEvent via roleOutputToEvent. 2. Delegates to processEvent. This is the bridge between role execution and lane state.

returnLane_flow.description: At terminal time (checkpointed state): 1. Checks lane is terminal via isTerminal(). 2. Emits LaneReturned event. 3. Calls binderService.finalizeBinder(). 4. Returns the finalized binder.

binder.role: Persistent evidence ledger per lane. Stores all artifacts, events, validation results, red team findings, repair history. Supports EventStore reconstruction.

methods.createBinder: (laneId, campaignId, mission, scope) â†’ Effect<string> â€” initializes in Ref<Map<string,Binder>> and records binder.created event
methods.getBinder: (laneId) â†’ Effect<Option<Binder>> â€” reads from in-memory Ref, falls back to EventStore reconstruction
methods.getBindersByCampaignId: (campaignId) â†’ Effect<Binder[]> â€” filters all binders by campaign
methods.addEvidence: (laneId, section, artifact) â†’ Effect<void,BinderError> â€” mutates binder in Ref and records binder.evidence event
methods.updateStatus: (laneId, status, terminalStatus?) â†’ Effect<void,BinderError> â€” mutates binder.status in Ref and records binder.status event
methods.finalizeBinder: (laneId) â†’ Effect<Binder,BinderError> â€” sets completedAt, computes artifactDigest, records binder.finalized event
methods.reconstructBinder: (laneId) â†’ Effect<Option<Binder>> â€” replays binder.* events from EventStore to rebuild binder from scratch

persistence_mechanism.primary_store: Ref<Map<string, Binder>> â€” in-memory Map, mutated on every operation
persistence_mechanism.durability: EventStore write-through for reconstruction. Every mutation also records a binder.created / binder.evidence / binder.status / binder.finalized event.
persistence_mechanism.reconstruction: reconstructBinder() queries EventStore for all binder.* events for a laneId, then replays them: binder.created initializes fields, binder.status mutates status, binder.evidence appends to sections, binder.finalized sets completedAt + artifactDigest.
persistence_mechanism.cache_miss: getBinder() checks Ref first. On miss, calls reconstructBinder() then caches result in Ref.

event_types_recorded.binder.created: on createBinder â€” stores laneId, campaignId, mission, scope
event_types_recorded.binder.evidence: on addEvidence â€” stores section + artifact/event ref
event_types_recorded.binder.status: on updateStatus â€” stores new status + optional terminalStatus
event_types_recorded.binder.finalized: on finalizeBinder â€” stores completedAt + artifactDigest

integrator.role: Multi-lane integration coordinator â€” merges binders after all lanes complete, detects conflicts, assigns merge strategies.

own_binder_interface.conversion: toIntegratorBinder() at line 199 converts canonical Binder â†’ integrator Binder by extracting files from executionEvents, diff from diffSummary, dependsOn from dependencyLaneIds

comparison_with_canonical_binder.integrator_focus: File-level changes (created/modified/deleted), type exports, dependency graph. Minimal subset for merge algorithms.
comparison_with_canonical_binder.canonical_focus: Full evidence chain: scout reports, architecture plans, critic reviews, validation results, red team findings, repair history. Complete artifact ledger.
comparison_with_canonical_binder.missing_from_integrator: scoutReports, architecturePlan, criticReviews, approvedPlan, validationResults, redTeamFindings, repairHistory, checkpointCommit, residualRisks, handoffSummary, terminalStatus â€” integrator only needs file-level diff + dependency info.
comparison_with_canonical_binder.shared_fields: laneId, executionEvents (via EventRef), dependsOn (= dependencyLaneIds), diff (via DiffSummary)

key_functions.topologicalSort: line 115 â€” Kahn's algorithm for dependency ordering, detects cycles
key_functions.collectSharedFiles: line 153 â€” identifies files touched by multiple lanes
key_functions.detectConflicts: line 166 â€” flags lanes that modify the same file
key_functions.assignMergeStrategy: line 175 â€” returns 'sequential_checkpoints'|'rebase'|'replay' based on shared file count
key_functions.constructContextPacket: line ~230 â€” builds the context fed to the integrator agent
key_functions.mergeLaneCheckpoints: line ~300 â€” executes git merge of all lane checkpoint SHAs
key_functions.detectContractDrift: line ~340 â€” compares exported API surfaces between lanes

predicates.role: Typed transition predicate engine â€” evaluates PredicateSpecs against runtime event ledgers, file memory, and claims.

predicate_resolvers.description: Record<string, PredicateResolver> mapping kind â†’ resolver function. Used by checkPredicate/checkTransitions.

check_transitions.description: Given an AgentStateMachineSpec and current state, evaluates all outgoing transitions in priority order. Returns first satisfied transition or null.

state_machine.role: Declarative state machine spec + deterministic reducer. Two machines: LANE_STATE_MACHINE and CAMPAIGN_STATE_MACHINE.

key_types.TransitionRule: { to: string, predicate: PredicateSpec, priority?: number }
key_types.AgentStateSpec: { allowedTools: string[], transitions: TransitionRule[], entryActions?: ActionSpec[], retryPolicy?: RetryPolicy }
key_types.AgentMachineSpec: { id: string, initial: string, version: string, states: Record<string,AgentStateSpec> }
key_types.RuntimeEvent: { type: string, timestamp: string?, payload?: Record<string,unknown> }
key_types.CampaignState: { currentState: string, events: RuntimeEvent[], transitionCount: number, stateHistory: StateTransition[], metadata: Record<string,unknown>, retryBudgets: Record<string,number> }

approved.note: Uses plan_approved predicate â€” same event type that got us here may also trigger transition out

failed.note: Not defined in LANE_STATE_MACHINE â€” handled by secretary's processEvent fallback

scoping.note: Extra state not in types.ts LaneState literals

full_state_paths.happy_path: created â†’ scouting â†’ scoped â†’ planning â†’ critic_review â†’ approved â†’ executing â†’ validating â†’ red_team â†’ historian â†’ checkpointed â†’ returned
full_state_paths.repair_loop: executing â†’ validating â†’ red_team â†’ repairing â†’ executing (retry_budget_remaining) OR blocked (repair_budget_exhausted)
full_state_paths.planning_loop: planning â†’ critic_review â†’ planning (plan_rejected loop, max unbounded)

evaluate_predicate.description: Heuristic pure-function predicate evaluator used by reduceCampaignState. Takes (PredicateSpec, RuntimeEvent[], CampaignState) â†’ boolean. Handles all predicate kinds including those missing from predicateResolvers.
evaluate_predicate.note: This is the SECOND predicate evaluation system. The typed resolvers in predicates.ts are the 'canonical' ones (with evidence), but reduceCampaignState uses this heuristic evaluator.

reduce_campaign_state.description: Pure deterministic reducer. Walks events in order, for each event checks all transitions from current state by priority, transitions on first satisfied predicate. Executes entry actions after each transition.

campaign_state_machine.states: created, scouting, scope_synthesis, lane_decomposition, lane_dispatch, waiting_for_lanes, integration_review, final_validation, push_ready, pushed, blocked, failed
campaign_state_machine.blocked_transitions: shared function returns [has_claim_conflictâ†’blocked, child_blockedâ†’blocked, scope_unsafeâ†’blocked] prepended to every state

mismatches_and_concerns.title: find_blocking predicate kind missing from PredicateSpec
mismatches_and_concerns.description: LANE_STATE_MACHINE red_team â†’ repairing transition uses predicate kind "finding_blocking" (line 153). This kind is NOT in the PredicateSpec type union in predicates.ts. evaluatePredicate in state-machine.ts handles it (line 379: case "finding_blocking": return events.some(e => e.type === EventName.FindingBlocking)), but the typed predicateResolvers map has NO entry for it. Calling checkPredicate() or checkTransitions() with this kind would throw at runtime.
mismatches_and_concerns.impact: If checkTransitions() from predicates.ts replaces evaluatePredicate, red_team â†’ repairing transition breaks.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: redteam_completed predicate kind missing from PredicateSpec
mismatches_and_concerns.description: LANE_STATE_MACHINE red_team â†’ historian transition uses predicate kind "redteam_completed" (line 154). NOT in PredicateSpec. evaluatePredicate handles it (lines 381-384: filters for EventName.RedteamCompleted, checks blockingFindings===0 in payload). predicateResolvers has NO entry â€” would throw.
mismatches_and_concerns.impact: If checkTransitions() replaces evaluatePredicate, red_team â†’ historian transition breaks.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: all_gates_pass predicate kind missing from predicateResolvers
mismatches_and_concerns.description: all_gates_pass is defined in PredicateSpec (line 58) but has NO entry in predicateResolvers map. evaluatePredicate handles it (checks EventName.GatesAllPassed). checkPredicate would throw.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: repair_budget_exhausted has STUB resolver in predicateResolvers
mismatches_and_concerns.description: The predicateResolvers entry for repair_budget_exhausted is a stub: (_spec, _ctx) => { console.warn('stub resolver'); return { satisfied: false } }. evaluatePredicate handles it correctly by checking state.retryBudgets. If code switches to checkPredicate, repairing â†’ blocked transition will never fire.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: Two predicate evaluation systems with diverging coverage
mismatches_and_concerns.description: evaluatePredicate (state-machine.ts:285) and checkPredicate/predicateResolvers (predicates.ts:696) are TWO SEPARATE predicate evaluators. evaluatePredicate is a heuristic pure-function used by reduceCampaignState. predicateResolvers is the 'typed evidence' engine used by checkPredicate/checkTransitions. They have different capability coverage and different code paths. The claim 'predicate evaluation uses typed evidence predicates' (state-machine.ts comment line 9: SM-003) is only partially true â€” reduceCampaignState uses the heuristic evaluator, not the typed one.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: toSMEvent uses raw strings instead of EventName constants
mismatches_and_concerns.description: The toSMEvent function in secretary.ts maps RuntimeEvent tags to raw strings like "scout.completed", "plan.produced", "redteam.finding". evaluatePredicate in state-machine.ts checks EventName.ScoutCompleted, EventName.PlanProduced, etc. They match by string value only â€” there's no type-level assurance that the strings emitted by toSMEvent are the same strings checked by evaluatePredicate. A typo in toSMEvent would silently break transitions.
mismatches_and_concerns.example: toSMEvent emits 'redteam.finding' (literal string) while evaluatePredicate uses EventName.RedteamFinding which is 'redteam.finding' â€” they match, but only by coincidence.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: RedTeamCompleted never emitted by roleOutputToEvent
mismatches_and_concerns.description: roleOutputToEvent maps the 'redteam' role to RedTeamComplete (which toSMEvent maps to 'redteam.finding'). The LANE_STATE_MACHINE red_team â†’ historian transition needs redteam_completed predicate which checks for EventName.RedteamCompleted ('redteam.completed'). The secretary type defines a RedTeamCompleted event with blockingFindings/totalFindings, but roleOutputToEvent NEVER emits it. This event must be emitted by a different code path (possibly the orchestrator or a post-red-team aggregation step). If that external code path is missing, the red_team â†’ historian transition will never fire.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: Approved â†’ executing transition uses plan_approved (self-loop risk)
mismatches_and_concerns.description: The approved state transitions to executing on plan_approved predicate (line 133). But the transition into approved was ALSO triggered by plan_approved. This means the same event batch that moves the lane from critic_review â†’ approved could also immediately move it to executing. The reduceCampaignState processes one event at a time and restarts the transition scan per event, so this should work â€” but it depends on the event stream having the right ordering.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: Scoping state not in types.ts LaneState literals
mismatches_and_concerns.description: LANE_STATE_MACHINE defines a 'scoping' state (line ~165) with transitions, but the official LaneState schema in types.ts (line 7-22) does not include 'scoping'. This means secretary.processEvent would use a state that fails schema validation.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: Failed state not in LANE_STATE_MACHINE â€” handled by secretary fallback
mismatches_and_concerns.description: LANE_STATE_MACHINE does NOT define a 'failed' state. The secretary's processEvent has special fallback handling: case Blocked â†’ 'blocked', case Failed â†’ 'failed', case Unblocked â†’ restore previous. The 'failed' state exists only as a secretary-side override, not as a first-class state machine state.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: HistorianComplete and CheckpointCreated both map to SessionCheckpoint
mismatches_and_concerns.description: EVENT_NAME_MAP maps both HistorianComplete and CheckpointCreated to EventName.SessionCheckpoint ('session.checkpoint'). The historian â†’ checkpointed transition uses event_exists with EventName.SessionCheckpoint. This means EITHER event triggers the same transition. Potentially redundant.

mismatches_and_concerns.title: LearningComplete and ScoutComplete both map to scout.completed
mismatches_and_concerns.description: Both LearningComplete and ScoutComplete map to 'scout.completed' in toSMEvent. The scouting â†’ scoped transition checks scout_completed predicate. This is a deliberate dual-entry path for backward compatibility with 'Learning' terminology vs 'Scout' terminology.

key_edges.secretary â†’ binder: imports BinderService, Binder (type), BinderLayer, BinderError, BinderLaneState

event_trace_for_happy_path.description: The sequence of secretary RuntimeEvent emissions and their effect on the state machine for a successful lane

steps.note: Lane creation, no SM transition yet

steps.event: (next event batch â€” plan_approved already fired)

steps.note: RedTeamCompleted NOT emitted by roleOutputToEvent â€” must come from external code

event_trace_for_repair_loop.description: The red_team â†’ repairing â†’ executing feedback loop

event_trace_for_blocking_paths.description: Paths where the lane can be blocked

entries.effect: planning â†’ planning (loop, not blocked). roleOutputToEvent for blocked critic returns CriticComplete(rejected), NOT Blocked.
secretary fallback: newState='blocked'. SM doesn't handle Blocked directly.
Campaign state â†’ blocked via blockedTransitions()
